consumer_rules: Do not infer blueprint completeness by reading this overlay file alone; resolve overlay plus base_plan first.
Use the loop snapshot for machine-readable gate state, not as the canonical phase tree.

notes: This overlay is the active entrypoint and activation shell; the canonical phase tree remains in the base plan referenced by base_plan.
This overlay keeps the repository blueprint truth active while leaving continuation in explicit manual mode.
The report-production and report-delivery waves remain part of active blueprint truth.
The ModelBus-backed AI assistance governance and runtime-bridge wave is now completed in repo truth through L16.8.
The release-window attestation follow-up wave is now completed in repo truth through L17.4.
The QNAIGC search-provider follow-up wave is now fully completed in repo truth through L18.4.
The feed-bundle and digest-delivery hardening wave is now fully completed in repo truth through L19.6.

This wave corrected the repo-read: DataPulse already had packs, feed exports, and digest builders, so the real gap was replayable feed_bundle semantics, deterministic digest payloads, prompt override order, shared onboarding defaults, and Telegram delivery hardening.

The L20 governance-snapshot decoupling wave is now completed in repo truth through L20.5, so resolver-owned runtime bundle roots and same-window evidence roots are no longer coupled to tracked out/* truth.
The L21 ModelBus repo-prep wave is now completed in repo truth through L21.3, preserving resolver-owned bundle-first semantics while keeping web_research non-public.

L21.1 and L21.2 are now landed in repo truth: public-surface runtime-hit and same-window attestation closure covers mission_suggest, triage_assist, claim_draft, delivery_summary, plus report_draft=verified_fail_closed.
The L22 console text-measurement hardening wave is now completed in repo truth through L22.4, leaving grapheme-safe truncation and dense single-line canvas-fit handling as the current baseline.
The L23 console text-overflow runtime-evidence wave remains completed in repo truth through L23.2, so the console-text lane itself stays closed unless a new evidence-backed text wave is opened.
The L24 console interaction-clarity wave is now completed in repo truth through L24.5.
The L25 workflow-first console simplification wave is now completed in repo truth through L25.5.
The L26 console modularity-and-traceability follow-up wave is now fully completed in repo truth through L26.5.
The L27 runtime-kernel and multi-surface projection wave is now completed in repo truth through L27.5.
L27 left DataPulse with a shared surface capability catalog, runtime introspection, and explicit reopen rules over the Reader-backed lifecycle kernel.

The L29 wave treats query intent, source planning, evidence stitching, and coverage-gap projection as an internal research substrate over the existing Reader-backed lifecycle and report-layer objects.
L29 does not publish a new public AI surface, does not admit report_draft, and does not reopen collector-count or provider-count growth as the primary storyline.

The L29 intent-aware research substrate wave is now completed in repo truth through L29.6, with machine-checkable routing audit fields, projection parity checks, and bounded reopen rules over planning regressions, provenance drift, and cross-surface contradiction.
The L30 public-apis feed screening and evidence projection seeds wave is now completed in repo truth through L30.3.
The L32 execution-control-plane activation wave is now completed in repo truth through the model-tier execution-profile contract.

The L33 console subtractive-convergence and first-load-reduction wave is now completed in repo truth through `L33.6`, so populated-workspace chrome compaction, request-scope hardening, hidden-dock enforcement, and restored-context browser acceptance now form the current console baseline.

The L34 console engineering governance wave is now in progress in repo truth: `L34.1` absorbed the extracted-console baseline and `L34.2` landed the domain-owned bundle split plus dedicated bootstrap fragment, so tracked console fragments and sorted-fragment loaders now form the admitted repo baseline without reopening framework or bundler migration debate.
The current `next_slice` is `L34.3` (`Pure-function JS unit coverage`), while `L34.4` stays blocked behind both the landed domain split and the remaining JS test harness coverage slice.

L27 kept Reader-backed lifecycle truth canonical, treated collectors as acquisition adapters, kept AI as a governed overlay, and did not reopen shell-order simplification or force a frontend-stack migration.
Operator review should treat any post-closeout truth drift as a coupling bug to remove rather than as evidence that L27 is still open or needs a parallel control plane.
